title: City of David and southern ridge excavations
summary: Modern excavation has documented dense occupation layers, water systems, and domestic remains across multiple biblical periods in Jerusalem's southern ridge.
debates: Exact city boundaries in each period
caution: Jerusalem is firmly identified, but individual strata and reconstructions remain under scholarly review.

title: Qumran caves and manuscript recovery
summary: The cave system near Qumran produced the Dead Sea Scrolls, reshaping textual criticism and Second Temple period study.
debates: relationship between the settlement and the scrolls
caution: The manuscripts are undisputed, but the social identity of the site is still debated.

title: Caesarea harbor and administrative city
summary: Excavations at Caesarea have documented Herodian harbor engineering, Roman public buildings, and the Latin Pilate inscription.
debates: site-level interpretation of some administrative areas
caution: The urban complex is secure, though the exact sequencing of harbor phases remains under discussion.

title: Lachish siege and destruction horizon
summary: Excavation at Lachish has produced one of the clearest Iron Age destruction horizons in Judah, alongside siege remains and ostraca.
debates: Precise sequencing of the siege levels
extent of the Assyrian vs. Babylonian horizon
caution: The site evidence is strong, but interpretive questions remain about the details of each destruction phase.

title: Megiddo stratigraphic sequence
summary: Megiddo's long stratigraphic sequence makes it a type site for Iron Age chronology and regional history.
debates: Correlating certain strata with biblical chronology
caution: The stratigraphy is secure, but matching strata to specific biblical events requires caution.

title: Babylon and the exile horizon
summary: Work in Babylon and its associated mound complex provides the archaeological background for the exile and Persian restoration setting.
debates: How to map biblical references onto shifting imperial districts
how much of the literary exile narrative is place-specific
caution: The city is secure, but biblical texts often refer to Babylon as an imperial center rather than a single excavation trench.

title: Nineveh palace and library excavations
summary: Excavations at Nineveh uncovered monumental palaces, reliefs, and the library of Ashurbanipal.
debates: How the palace reliefs should be read alongside biblical narrative
caution: The Assyrian imperial context is well established, but the literary framing in biblical texts is not an excavation report.

title: Ephesus urban excavations
summary: Ephesus preserves a dense Roman cityscape that illuminates the Pauline and Revelation settings in Asia Minor.
debates: exact relationship of some Christian memory locations to first-century buildings
caution: The city is secure, though some features have shifted through time and later restoration.
